mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS value 134041.0 at t-1mo indicates strong employment
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above a certain threshold, it signals potential erosion of purchasing power and increased pressure on corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as the Fed is likely to tighten.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL value 198.5 at t-1mo is above 195 threshold
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL is above 195 but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of robust retail sales and low unemployment indicates strong consumer demand and economic health. This dual strength boosts corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS 351461.0 and UNRATE 5.1 at t-1mo suggest strong consumer conditions
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth is positive and UNRATE is below 5.5%, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 45% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates increase the cost of capital and discount future earnings more aggressively, leading to downward pressure on equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS value 3.62 at t-1mo is part of an ongoing tightening cycle
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises as specified, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances within a tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A robust housing market, indicated by high housing starts, stimulates related industries like construction and finance, boosting overall economic activity and corporate profits, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST value 2009.0 at t-2mo indicates a strong housing market
failure_condition: If HOUST is above 1800 but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and mining sectors, indicating strong supply-side economic activity that supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO value 118.016 at t-1mo indicates ongoing industrial growth
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of robust GDP growth and low unemployment signifies a strong and expanding economy. This environment fosters corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 11089.2 at t-6mo and UNRATE 5.1 at t-1mo suggest economic strength
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is positive and UNRATE is below 5.5%, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 40% of observed instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a critical threshold, it signals weakening labor market conditions, reduced consumer confidence, and potential economic contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE value 5.1 at t-1mo is near a critical threshold
failure_condition: If UNRATE is above 5.5% for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances within a slowdown regime.
